cases.name: Workspace becomes ready in desktop GUI
steps: Run GUI smoke through the supported app entrypoint
Inspect bridge readiness and workspace state
Capture screenshot or trace plus console state
expected: Bridge is ready before the UI is judged
No mock fallback hides a bridge failure
cases.risk: UI reports readiness without runtime support

cases.name: Tauri command contract is synchronized
steps: Compare frontend invocations, Rust registrations, catalog, DevBridge, and mocks
Inspect generated runtime clients or catalog output
expected: Every user-visible command exists in all required sides
Mocks do not define commands missing from real bridge
cases.risk: GUI calls a command that only exists in mock or catalog

cases.name: Browser runtime site adapter smoke
cases.target: browser runtime panel and site adapter
steps: Run browser-runtime smoke or Playwright continuation
Open the runtime panel and execute a site-adapter fixture
Capture screenshot, DOM/a11y snapshot, console/network log, and bridge event
expected: Panel shows real adapter state and not stale mock data
Console/network log has no unwaived error
Bridge event matches the visible runtime state
cases.risk: browser runtime panel hides adapter or bridge failure

cases.name: Provider settings WebView regression
steps: Run focused component or browser test for provider settings
Exercise empty, saved, invalid, and permission-denied states
Capture console output and stable regression report
expected: Invalid or denied state is visible to the user
No console error is hidden by optimistic UI
cases.risk: settings UI shows saved state that runtime rejects

evidence_policy: GUI pass requires smoke or interaction evidence, not only static checks. Bridge-backed UI proof must name whether it used real backend, fake provider, or mock bridge.
